title: Sleep and recovery
source: Journal of Clinical Medicine review (MDPI); HPRC
text: Deep, slow-wave sleep is one of the body's main recovery windows: growth hormone peaks, muscle repair and protein synthesis step up, and glycogen stores are replenished. Too little sleep raises inflammatory markers and cortisol and lowers strength, endurance and reaction time. Most adults need about 7 to 9 hours, and people in heavy training may need more.
